text:
If you're planning around the heat, today's actually your easier day, mostly sunny with a high near 87 and lower humidity than what's coming. There's a Heat Advisory already posted, but that's really aimed at Tuesday, when highs push toward 96 and the heat index climbs as high as 102. Get your outdoor stuff done today or early tomorrow.

The lake's in good shape through this evening, south wind 5 to 15 knots, waves 1 to 2 feet, water sitting at a pleasant 75. Tonight stays quiet with just a slight chance of a shower before 7. Tuesday's the one to watch closely, not for the heat alone but for what follows it: a sharp cold front moving through Tuesday evening into the overnight hours with strong to severe thunderstorms, damaging wind gusts the main threat, with an outside chance of an isolated tornado, especially north toward the border. Rain chances jump to 80 percent overnight with a possible half inch.

Enjoy the water today, good conditions right through sunset. Tomorrow, get your outdoor time in early and be somewhere sturdy by evening.
packet:
OBSERVED (2026-07-13T12:00:00+00:00): Clear, 72F feels 71F, humidity 57%, wind S 9 mph
ALERTS: Heat Advisory issued July 13 at 2:22AM EDT until July 14 at 8:00PM EDT by NWS Burlington VT
NWS FORECAST:
  Today: Mostly sunny, with a high near 87. South wind 6 to 10 mph.
  Tonight: A slight chance of rain showers before 7pm. Partly cloudy, with a low around 69. South wind 3 to 7 mph. Chance of precipitation is 20%.
  Tuesday: Mostly sunny, with a high near 96. Heat index values as high as 102. Southwest wind 8 to 15 mph, with gusts as high as 25 mph. New rainfall amounts less than a tenth of an inch possible.
  Tuesday Night: A chance of showers and thunderstorms between 7pm and 11pm, then showers and thunderstorms. Mostly cloudy, with a low around 72. Heat index values as high as 101. West wind 7 to 13 mph, with gusts as high as 24 mph. Chance of precipitation is 80%. New rainfall amounts between a quarter and half of an inch possible.
AFD KEY MESSAGES (forecaster's reasoning, issued 2026-07-13T10:45:00+00:00):
  1. Heat will begin to build, with a Heat Advisory posted for most all of Vermont and northern New York on Tuesday. Some localized fire weather concerns are possible.
  2. A sharp cold front is expected to produce strong to severe showers and thunderstorms Tuesday evening and overnight. A few storms may be capable of significant severe weather hazards.
  3. Seasonable temperatures are generally expected for the latter half of the week into the weekend, with a few chances for showers possible.
AFD WHAT CHANGED: As of 229 AM EDT Monday... A Heat Advisory has been issued across much of Vermont and northern New York for Tuesday afternoon. The Storm Prediction Center has expanded the forecast region of severe weather in our region, and upgraded areas along the International Border to an Enhanced Risk (Level 3 of 5).
LAKE (broad waters):
  Today: South winds 5 to 15 knots. Partly sunny. Waves 1 to 2 feet.
  Tonight: South winds 10 knots or less, increasing to 10 to 15 knots. Partly cloudy. A slight chance of showers in the evening. Waves 1 foot or less.
  Tuesday: South winds 10 to 15 knots, becoming southwest. Partly sunny. Waves 1 to 3 feet, subsiding to 1 foot or less.
  Tuesday Night: Southwest winds 10 to 15 knots, becoming west. Showers likely with a chance of thunderstorms. Some thunderstorms may produce gusty winds and heavy rainfall. Waves 1 foot or less.
LAKE GAGE: water 75F, level 96.45 ft (normal)
MODEL SPREAD (where forecasts diverge):
  2026-07-13: highs [GFS 89, Euro 90, ICON 89] spread 1F; precip chance [GFS 10%, Euro 12%, ICON 5%]
  2026-07-14: highs [GFS 96, Euro 97, ICON 99] spread 3F; precip chance [GFS 60%, Euro 12%, ICON 33%]
  2026-07-15: highs [GFS 82, Euro 86, ICON 84] spread 4F; precip chance [GFS 60%, Euro 75%, ICON 48%]
AIR: AQI 30 Good (PM2.5) — On Monday, July 13th,  expect moderate air quality in Vermont, as smoke from Canadian wildfires continues to circulate around the New England region.
SUN: rise 2026-07-13T05:20-04:00, set 2026-07-13T20:36-04:00, UV max 7.45
WEATHER UNDERGROUND / WEATHER.COM:
  high 89, low 69: Partly cloudy. Highs in the upper 80s and lows in the upper 60s.
  high 97, low 71: Times of sun and clouds. Highs in the upper 90s and lows in the low 70s.
WCAX METEOROLOGIST: Tuesday is a First Alert Weather Day with hazy, hot and humid conditions expected with highs ranging from the low to mid-90s. Those areas that reach the mid-90s could have a heat index around 100 degrees. Also, we’re watching a cold front that could bring a few severe thunderstorms during the evening or overnight. Right now, the northern portion of the area near the Canadian border is under an enhanced risk or level 3/5 for severe weather. The threat level drops the further south you go. These storms could have a number of potential threats, the biggest by far being strong and gusty wind. The strongest of the cells could feature gusts upwards of 70+ MPH. While high winds are the biggest threat, there could be a bit of potential rotation in the atmosphere, meaning a few isolated spin-up tornadoes are possible. To go along with it, some cells could feature large hail and heavy downpours.
NBC5 FIRST WARNING (2026-07-12T22:24:00Z): More clouds expected Monday, a shower possible — Intense heat Tuesday may spark off evening and overnight severe storms
